scope: Durable provider-call idempotency for authenticated tutor POST and administrator credential test/replace. Credential reveal is deliberately excluded and remains a fresh-MFA, reason, audit, and learner-notification ceremony on every access.

implementation.canonicalHash: SHA-256 of a deterministic, key-sorted JSON projection. Tutor input uses the sanitized message plus course, skill, and nullable thread. Administrator input uses credential, learner, action, reason, and replacement material only when replacing; plaintext is never stored in the receipt.
implementation.storedResponse: Only a JSON-object body, HTTP status, and completion time; maximum serialized body is 262144 bytes.
implementation.concurrency: A PostgreSQL unique claim selects one executor. Exact concurrent retries poll the durable processing receipt and replay the completed response. The executor stores completion before returning HTTP.
implementation.mismatch: The same owner/action/request UUID with a different canonical hash returns 409 IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_REUSED before provider or mutation work.
implementation.lostResponse: A completed exact retry returns the original safe status/body with X-Idempotent-Replay=true and does not re-enter rate limiting, provider routing, credential mutation, audit, notification, model-call, or chat writes.
implementation.indeterminateFailure: A processing receipt is never automatically reclaimed because the provider may already have received the operation. Wait timeout returns retryable 503 rather than risking a duplicate provider call.
implementation.client: Tutor and administrator credential test/replace clients generate UUIDs and retry one transport exception with the byte-equivalent payload and UUID. Application/provider error responses are not blindly retried.
implementation.retention: Tutor response copies are deleted in bounded batches at the existing 12-calendar-month raw-chat cutoff. Credential test/replace receipts are administrator/security records with no automatic launch purge and cascade with owning-account deletion.

securityInvariants.tutorRedaction: The immediate learner message is sanitized once before canonical hashing, provider transmission, model-call hashing, or chat persistence.
securityInvariants.credentialSecrets: The receipt has no secret/plaintext column, ordinary response contains no credential, repository secret scan passes, and reveal responses never enter this receipt system.
securityInvariants.credentialCas: Existing active-row owner/key-version/exact-updated-at provider outcome compare-and-set remains unchanged. Administrator service key-version checks remain inside its locked mutation transaction.
securityInvariants.authorization: Receipt owner is derived only from the authenticated session. Existing tutor thread owner/status gates and administrator role/fresh-MFA/learner binding remain in place.
databaseConstraints: owner foreign key with delete cascade
HTTP status range and response byte-size checks

realPostgresConcurrency.result: 1 file passed; 3 tests passed; migrations through 0025 applied successfully twice
coverage: two concurrent tutor executions make one synthetic provider call, one model_call, one thread, and one user/assistant message pair
completed lost-response tutor replay returns the original call/thread response without execution
actual administrator credential test and replace each validate the provider once under concurrent duplicate requests
actual administrator service persists one requested/completed audit pair, one requested/completed in-app notice pair, one requested/completed email pair, and one credential mutation
replacement retains key-version/CAS behavior and receipt JSON excludes replacement plaintext

verification.lint: passed with 0 errors and 0 warnings in the final repository gate

remainingLimitations: No upstream provider transaction can make an unknowable host crash after provider acceptance but before durable completion replayable. The application deliberately leaves that receipt processing and refuses automatic re-execution; operator diagnosis is required.
This focused artifact is not a production live-provider quality/terms/availability test.
The final repository-wide gate and 19-file/87-test PostgreSQL suite passed, but this artifact remains local evidence rather than a live-provider or deployed-host proof.
